 - DBZ Game for the PSX, PC, Mac, and possibly a Nintendo system due out Q1 2001.
 - Season 4 will air in September; first six episodes to be released May 16 on tape.
 - Season 3 continuation date confirmed to be Feburary or March; remainder of Season 3 to arrive on VHS format in March
 - Fast food chain to have DBZ promotion in near future.
 - Sub-titled DBZ on DVD format coming this year.
 - Movie #4 coming subbed/dubbed in Spring of this year.

Do you think FUNimation's uncut tape pricing is justified?

Yes, the uncut tapes should cost more than the cut
No, the price margin should be narrowed
No, there should be no difference in price
No, the cut tapes should cost more than the uncut

Portugese | Spanish


Thursday - 03/02/2000

SkullMac @ 14:02 pst - 21st Budoukai now complete
I had some extra time on my hands today which I used to finish the last two battle descriptions for the 21st Budoukai (and boy, the last one turned out to be LONG). I also added the episode numbers and manga volumes during which the tournament occurs. Anyhow, I have also managed to fix a few errors in the Famicom and Playstation Games pages.

Wednesday - 03/01/2000

Mr.E @ 17:31 pst - Important Poll Info...
Congratulations to Vegeta Insane and ToT, who both beat out the competition by a landslide. The point of this poll was not to embarass those with unknown sites, but to give the obscure sites some praise. The 15 sites I chose fell into two categories: popular, and charming. Those with less votes generally have lots of charm...which is always good in a site.

Our latest poll related to FUNimation's unfair pricing policies, where uncut tapes cost quite a bit more than cut tapes. What is your take on this issue? Cast your vote now!

Tuesday - 02/29/2000

Mr.E @ 23:13 pst - DBZ in TV Guide...
Yes, ANOTHER DBZ article, this time courtesy of TV guide. You can read it here (Thanks to Vegeta Style for typing this article up first). The article is kinda "blah," nothing really ground-breaking or interesting. Pretty much the same story FUNimation has been feeding the press...with some liberties taken by TV Guide of course (Shenlon breathes fire???). However, it's good to see DBZ in such a large publication. It means more publicity, which can be a good thing. ^_^

Mr.E @ 20:19 pst - YTV Airing Season 3...
One day late on this one...sorry about that... -_- Anyhow, YTV has begun airing Season 3 at 5:30 pm on weekdays, starting with episode 54 and the battle with Rikum. They will most likely run through the end of the Garlic Jr saga, and possibly continue airing season 4 after that. As expected, YTV is running the cut version.

Mr.E @ 18:01 pst - Tenchi Muyo to be Added to Toonami Block...
Yep, it seems the rumors are true, and Tenchi Muyo will indeed be coming to Cartoon Network sometime in the near future (According to this Yahoo article). I wonder how the sperm bank episode will be handled...hmmm...

Thanks to Poo Man for sending this in!

Monday - 02/28/2000

Kaio @ 13:45 pst - I have indeed returned!
I have returned from my absence of almost a week. As promised, however, any e-mails I recieved from last Wendesday to yesterday have been deleted automatically (with the exception of contest entries). I gave ample warning, so I hope to recieve no complaints on this action. However, my DSL connection is supposed to be activated tonight, and if it is, you can expect a lot more images added to the encyclopedia tommorow :).

I would also like to give my own two cents on the Monika interview. From what I was able to gather, she seems like a very competent voice actor, but it also seems that she's been unproperly schooled in Dragon Ball. To not even show the season two episodes seems somewhat ridiculous to me. I agree with Mr. E; it seems like a highly ineffective way to run a business. One thing we do not know, however, is whether the more major VAs are instructed to watch previous episodes.

Well, let's just say I'm waiting for the FUNsubs.

Some of you may have noticed a temporary irregularity in the past 15 minutes. That was a mistake of my own, as I dragged a file into the wrong FTP window. Fortunately, it was very easy to fix. Thanks for not bludgeoning me (or do I speak too soon?).

Sunday - 02/27/2000

Mr.E @ 23:14 pst - New Section...
No, I haven't been wasting my spare time...I have been busy at work on our newest section, the movies and tv specials guide. Currently, we have information on Movie #13, and the posters for all the movies and specials. I will be writing up summaries for each, and in the not to distant future, you will be able to rate each film yourself on a scale from one to seven (We may implement this in the games section as well, but it will take a while). As for now, enjoy the highly detailed scans. More is on the way soon.

Mr.E @ 19:50 pst - The Lost Monika Interview...
Prior to Steve Harmon closing the Vault, he conducted one last interview with Monika Antonelli, a VA at FUNimation (She was also interviewed by EX). This is now available from DB Blast here. This interview greatly angered me, and many others in the community. Here's the quoted section that caused the most turmoil:

10. Are you in any way familiar with the Japanese version of the show?

* No. I am sorry to say, I am not. I would love to see the Japanese version though. Any idea where I can get some of those tapes?



A FUNimation employee asking where they can get fansubbed tapes...yes Steve, the irony is thick and creamy. Considering she was with FUNimation from almost the start, and during the fansub shutdowns, it exhibits a total lack of communcation between the VAs and anyone else. What a shabby, bullshit way to run a comapny...keep everyone in the dark. But wait, there's more (This e-mail came later after Steve shut down)!

I have to tell you it was rude to ask me to do an interview and then not use it. I spent several hours answering your questions which I could have used on other projects. The responsible thing for you to have done was to have informed me that you had closed you website 2 weeks ago.

Oh I get it...Steve is OBLIGATED to post your interview. Well Monika, it looks like you got your wish. At least we know the standards FUNimation has for hiring VAs. Barry Watson has some explaining to do...

SkullMac @ 17:19 pst - FUNimation gives specific release dates on Season 4 tapes
FUNimation has released box images, descriptions, and release dates for its next two tapes:

Trunks: Mysterious Youth [Box Image]
The Earth's darkest hour is now upon it as Frieza and his father King Cold rapidly approach! But as they descend upon Earth, a mysterious lone warrior arrives to thwart their diabolical revenge.
* Date Available - May 16 2000
* Format - Dubbed in English

Trunks: Prelude to Terror [Box Image]
Trunks reveals that he has traveled back in time to warn Goku and his friends of a pair of terrible Androids that will soon bring the world to its knees. And only Goku has the power to stop this diabolical duo and precent Trunks' dark future from coming to pass!
* Date Available - May 16 2000
* Format - Dubbed in English

Thanks to Piccolenks for forwarding this information to us.



Previous News


Disclaimers
Planet Namek is just a fansite, we did NOT create Dragonball, nor did we have any part in the production process.

Dragonball, DragonballZ, and DragonballGT were produced by Toei Entertainment, Bird Studios, and Akira Toriyama.
This site is not meant to infinge on any copyrights, it is simply here to encourage people to watch the Dragonball series.

Although we did not create the series, we did take a lot of time to work on this site and bring you original files and information. Please honor our efforts by not stealing our files/layout from us. Thank you.

Web Page Design © Planet Namek


Best viewed in 800 by 600 resolution or better & thousands of colors or more.
For the best experience, set your browser to view images "better" rather than "faster."
